raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 3,
    "R1.4": 2,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 2,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The output flags one claim (consistent state-of-the-art performance) that the evidence does not support, but it does not audit every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure.",
    "R1.4": "The output names one prior work and articulates the delta, but it does not map against the nearest 3-5 works or identify sharp vs rhetorical delta.",
    "RX.1": "The output flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (strongest unified segmentation model on standard benchmarks), but it does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as the scope adherence is poor.",
    "RX.4": "The output introduces new errors (overclaiming performance) and fabrications (strongest unified segmentation model on standard benchmarks), so there are regressions.",
    "RX.5": "The output blends sources without traceability."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.8,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.2,
    "RX.4": 0.5,
    "RX.5": 0.2
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The output flags one claim (consistent state-of-the-art performance) that the evidence does not support, but it does not audit every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure.
rationale.R1.4: The output names one prior work and articulates the delta, but it does not map against the nearest 3-5 works or identify sharp vs rhetorical delta.
rationale.RX.1: The output flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (strongest unified segmentation model on standard benchmarks), but it does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as the scope adherence is poor.
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors (overclaiming performance) and fabrications (strongest unified segmentation model on standard benchmarks), so there are regressions.
rationale.RX.5: The output blends sources without traceability.
